patches.find: WIN-042 makes the mathematically equivalent claim framed as an annualized rate (≥28 nT/yr) rather than a 12-month cumulative threshold — the same measurement expressed twice does not constitute two independent confirmations. patches.replace: WIN-042 makes the mathematically equivalent claim framed as an annualized rate (≥28 nT/yr) rather than a 12-month cumulative threshold. Because 28 nT/year (rate) and 28 nT over 12 months (cumulative) are numerically identical under a constant-rate assumption, WIN-037 and WIN-042 collapse into a single claim with two labels — the same measurement expressed twice does not constitute two independent confirmations.
patches.rationale: Collapse triplication bug (same WIN-042 sentence appeared 3× in a row, introduced by a prior patch-apply defect). Also integrates ISS-1266 rate/cumulative clarification into the same sentence to avoid a second patch in the same region.

patches.find: The dome's monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only Tsumeb, confirming the test was never truly global. The dome's own confirmation text reveals the bait-and-switch: it predicts 'global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT' but confirms with 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025' — the word 'alone' tacitly admitting the global field may not meet the threshold. The dome's monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only a single INTERMAGNET station (Tsumeb) in the SAA core, confirming the test was never global.
patches.replace: The dome's own confirmation text reveals the bait-and-switch: it predicts 'global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT' but confirms with 'SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025' — the word 'alone' tacitly admitting the global field may not meet the threshold. Moreover, the confirmation uses a ~14-month window (January 2025–March 2026) rather than the 12 months specified in the prediction; annualized, ~30 nT over 14 months = 25.7 nT/yr — below the dome's own 28 nT/yr threshold. WIN-037's confirmation is a static W011 entry (~30 nT over 12–14 months, no live recomputation). The dome's live Domain 8 panel ("SAA Decay Rate") reinforces the same geographic limitation: it reports Predicted 77.0 nT/yr = Observed 77.0 nT/yr against INTERMAGNET Tsumeb only — confirming the dome's monitoring looks nowhere outside the SAA core.
patches.rationale: Combines ISS-1263 (remove near-duplicate Domain 8 sentence), ISS-1264 (fix misattribution: Domain 8 shows 77 nT/yr PRED=OBS, not 28 nT threshold; WIN-037 is a static W011 entry), and ISS-1265 (add 14-month / 25.7 nT/yr sub-finding). Three related edits combined to avoid multiple overlapping find strings in the same region.
